name: kube_proxy
explanation: kube-proxy exposes Prometheus-format metrics on the fixed path `/metrics` at TCP port 10249 (the `metricsBindAddress` default), and a health endpoint at `/healthz` on port 10256. The check is an OpenMetricsBaseCheck subclass that hardcodes `http://localhost:10249/metrics` as its default and even derives the health URL from the prometheus URL automatically. Discovery just needs to find a kube-proxy pod and probe port 10249. This is the standard in-cluster control-plane pattern (O) — when authentication is required, the Agent's pod ServiceAccount token is auto-injected; in the default insecure setup, no credentials are needed at all.
notes: No auto_conf.yaml ships, but the port (10249) and path (`/metrics`) are universal Kubernetes defaults. Pattern O applies.
